utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format contains 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, and total_budget. The final property solution includes all six items. Each is fully specified: (1) property includes type, price, currency, features, fees, location, move-in date; (2) financing includes method, bridge_financing, approval timeline; (3) legal_services includes provider, cost, currency, service_level, status; (4) moving_services includes provider_type, cost, currency, services_included, special_support; (5) setup_services includes utility_package, cost, currency, features, setup_timeline_days; (6) total_budget includes itemized costs, estimated total, currency. Each item is complete and detailed according to expectations.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final property solution shows a purchase of a '2-bedroom accessible bungalow/condo' priced at CAD 695,000 in Burlington. Among the ground-truth purchase options, the closest and most relevant match is the 'Family House' (CAD 650K, rating 9) because it fits the budget range (650K–750K), suits retirees, and includes multiple accessibility and lifestyle features. The financing method is 'Cash purchase using home sale proceeds,' which corresponds to 'Cash Purchase' in the ground-truth financing_solution.purchase_financing with a rating of 10. The legal service provider’s cost is CAD 800 with basic coverage, matching 'Basic Legal Package' (rating 8). The moving service costs CAD 650 and is described as a 'Senior downsizing mover,' aligning with 'Standard Moving' (rating 7). The setup services cost CAD 280 and include utilities and internet, which best match 'Premium Utilities' (rating 9). Therefore, the mapping corresponds to: Family House (9), Cash Purchase (10), Basic Legal Package (8), Standard Moving (7), and Premium Utilities (9).
